exercises.title: Cache hit/miss sequence
exercises.instructions:
Two-level cache: L1 Caffeine (30s TTL), L2 Redis (5min TTL), DB fallback.
Requests for product 'PROD-001' at T=0, T=10, T=35, T=6min.
For each: which cache level is hit? What is returned? Which caches are populated?
exercises.expected_solution:
T=0 (first request):
  L1: miss. L2: miss. DB: HIT (5ms).
  Populate: Redis with 5min TTL, Caffeine with 30s TTL.
  Returned: product from DB.

T=10 (10 seconds later, within L1 TTL):
  L1: HIT (0.1ms). No Redis or DB access.
  Returned: product from Caffeine.

T=35 (35 seconds later, L1 expired but L2 valid):
  L1: miss (expired after 30s). L2: HIT (Redis, ~1ms).
  Populate: Caffeine again with 30s TTL.
  Returned: product from Redis.

T=6min (6 minutes, both L1 and L2 expired):
  L1: miss. L2: miss (expired after 5min). DB: HIT.
  Populate: Redis 5min + Caffeine 30s.
  Returned: product from DB.

test_cases.expected:
T=0 (first request):
  L1: miss. L2: miss. DB: HIT (5ms).
  Populate: Redis with 5min TTL, Caffeine with 30s TTL.
  Returned: product from DB.

T=10 (10 seconds later, within L1 TTL):
  L1: HIT (0.1ms). No Redis or DB access.
  Returned: product from Caffeine.

T=35 (35 seconds later, L1 expired but L2 valid):
  L1: miss (expired after 30s). L2: HIT (Redis, ~1ms).
  Populate: Caffeine again with 30s TTL.
  Returned: product from Redis.

T=6min (6 minutes, both L1 and L2 expired):
  L1: miss. L2: miss (expired after 5min). DB: HIT.
  Populate: Redis 5min + Caffeine 30s.
  Returned: product from DB.

hints: L1 TTL=30s, L2 TTL=5min=300s
L1 hit: no L2 or DB access
L1 miss + L2 hit: repopulate L1 from L2

exercises.title: Complete the Caffeine cache configuration
exercises.instructions: Fill in for a product catalog cache (max 1000 entries, 30s write expiry, stats tracking).
hints: maximumSize: evicts LRU when full
recordStats(): enables cache.stats() for hit/miss counts

exercises.title: Build a simple L1 Caffeine cache
exercises.instructions:
Write ProductL1Cache @Service:
1. Caffeine cache: max 500, expire 60s, recordStats
2. get(id) → L1 or null
3. put(id, product)
4. hitRate() method using cache.stats()
5. @SpringBootTest: verify hit rate increases after second access
hints: recordStats(): enables cache.stats() hit/miss tracking
hitRate(): hits/(hits+misses) — should be 2/3=0.67 after 2 hits + 1 miss
getIfPresent: null on miss (doesn't load from source)

exercises.title: Cache-aside with L1 + L2 + DB
exercises.instructions:
Write complete cache-aside pattern:
1. L1 Caffeine check first (0.1ms)
2. L2 Redis check on L1 miss (1ms)
3. DB fallback on L2 miss (5ms)
4. Populate L1+L2 on DB hit
5. Metrics: counter by level
hints: l1.put on L2 hit: populate Caffeine from Redis result
l1 + L2 put on DB hit: populate both caches
Counter by 'level' tag: splits hits by L1/L2/DB in Prometheus

exercises.title: Cache invalidation on write
exercises.instructions:
Write ProductWriteThroughService:
1. updateProduct(): write to DB, invalidate L1 and L2
2. Why delete-before-write (not after)?
3. @SpringBootTest: update invalidates cache, next read goes to DB
hints: Delete before write: prevents caching stale value during concurrent read+write
l1.invalidate(key): removes the key from Caffeine
redis.delete(): removes from Redis (all instances see the invalidation)

exercises.title: Cache stampede protection with Redis lock
exercises.instructions:
Write StampedeProtectedCache:
1. On L1+L2 miss: acquire Redis lock (SETNX)
2. Only one thread fetches from DB
3. Others wait for lock, then read from L2
4. Explain: what happens without this protection
hints: setIfAbsent: Redis SETNX — returns true only for the one thread that wins the lock
Lock TTL (10s): if holding thread crashes, lock auto-expires
50ms sleep: wait for DB loader to populate cache, then re-read

exercises.title: Cache hit rate Gauge via Caffeine stats
exercises.instructions:
Register Caffeine cache stats as Micrometer Gauges:
1. Gauge for hit rate
2. Gauge for miss rate
3. Gauge for cache size
4. @SpringBootTest: verify gauges registered, hit rate increases
hints: recordStats(): REQUIRED for cache.stats() to work
Gauge with double method reference: lazy — evaluated on each scrape
2 hits + 1 miss = hitRate 0.67 > 0.5

exercises.title: Spring @Cacheable with Redis + Caffeine
exercises.instructions:
Configure Spring Cache with two-level provider:
1. CacheManager with Caffeine (L1) as primary
2. @Cacheable on product lookup method
3. @CacheEvict on product update
4. application.yml cache config
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): cacheable works, evict clears
hints: @EnableCaching: required on @SpringBootApplication or @Configuration
@Cacheable: proxy-based — self-invocation bypasses it!
verify(repo, times(1)): confirms cache prevented 2nd DB call

exercises.title: Cache warming on startup
exercises.instructions:
Write CacheWarmupService:
1. @EventListener(ApplicationStartedEvent): pre-load top 100 merchants
2. Load from DB into Caffeine + Redis
3. Log warm-up progress and completion time
4. @SpringBootTest: warm-up completes in < 2s
hints: @EventListener(ApplicationStartedEvent.class): runs after full context ready
warmUp in startup: eliminates cold start latency for first requests
estimatedSize() >= 90: Caffeine may report slightly less due to async eviction

exercises.title: Cache TTL strategy trade-offs
exercises.instructions:
For each data type, choose the best TTL for L1 and L2:
1. Product price (changes multiple times/day)
2. Merchant config (changes rarely, maybe once/week)
3. Exchange rate (changes every second)
4. User profile (changes on user action)
5. Payment status (immutable after finalization)
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Product price (changes multiple times/day):
   L1: 30 seconds (users may see slightly stale price briefly — acceptable)
   L2: 2 minutes (shared source of truth between instances)
   Invalidation: explicit delete on price update

2. Merchant config (changes rarely, once/week):
   L1: 5 minutes (low update rate — stale config for 5min acceptable)
   L2: 30 minutes (rarely changes → cache aggressively)
   Invalidation: on config update event → delete L1 + L2

3. Exchange rate (changes every second):
   L1: DO NOT CACHE (changes faster than any useful TTL)
   L2: 5 seconds MAX (with explicit refresh job every 1-2 seconds)
   Better: WebSocket push from exchange rate service → update cache on push
   Or: poll every 500ms from a local in-memory store (not Redis)

4. User profile (changes on user action):
   L1: 2 minutes (user won't notice 2min stale on their own profile)
   L2: 10 minutes
   Invalidation: on profile update → delete L1 + L2

5. Payment status (immutable after finalization):
   Finalized payments (COMPLETED, FAILED, REFUNDED): INFINITE TTL
   L1: 1 hour (evict via LRU, not TTL)
   L2: 24 hours or no expiry
   Reason: payment status never changes after finalization → no staleness risk
   Exception: PENDING payments → do NOT cache (may change every few seconds)

test_cases.expected:
1. Product price (changes multiple times/day):
   L1: 30 seconds (users may see slightly stale price briefly — acceptable)
   L2: 2 minutes (shared source of truth between instances)
   Invalidation: explicit delete on price update

2. Merchant config (changes rarely, once/week):
   L1: 5 minutes (low update rate — stale config for 5min acceptable)
   L2: 30 minutes (rarely changes → cache aggressively)
   Invalidation: on config update event → delete L1 + L2

3. Exchange rate (changes every second):
   L1: DO NOT CACHE (changes faster than any useful TTL)
   L2: 5 seconds MAX (with explicit refresh job every 1-2 seconds)
   Better: WebSocket push from exchange rate service → update cache on push
   Or: poll every 500ms from a local in-memory store (not Redis)

4. User profile (changes on user action):
   L1: 2 minutes (user won't notice 2min stale on their own profile)
   L2: 10 minutes
   Invalidation: on profile update → delete L1 + L2

5. Payment status (immutable after finalization):
   Finalized payments (COMPLETED, FAILED, REFUNDED): INFINITE TTL
   L1: 1 hour (evict via LRU, not TTL)
   L2: 24 hours or no expiry
   Reason: payment status never changes after finalization → no staleness risk
   Exception: PENDING payments → do NOT cache (may change every few seconds)

hints: Immutable data: cache indefinitely
Frequently changing: short TTL or no TTL + push invalidation
Always ask: what's the consequence of serving 30s stale data?

exercises.title: Complete Flipkart product cache service
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Flipkart product caching:
1. L1 Caffeine (1000, 30s) + L2 Redis (5min)
2. Hit rate gauges for each level
3. Cache warming @EventListener
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): L1 hit after 2nd access, hit rate > 50%
hints: @Import(FlipkartCacheConfig.class): makes the Cache bean available in test
2 puts + 2 gets = hitRate 1.0 (all accesses are hits after put)
Gauge with Cache::stats().hitRate(): lazy evaluation per Prometheus scrape

exercises.title: Write-through cache for merchant config
exercises.instructions:
Write MerchantConfigCache with write-through:
1. get(): L1 → L2 → DB
2. update(): write to DB AND L2 simultaneously (write-through)
3. Compare: write-through vs cache-aside write
4. @SpringBootTest: update reflects immediately in get()
hints: Caffeine.get(key, mappingFn): loads from DB on miss (no explicit null check needed)
Write-through: l1.put() in update() — immediately visible to all threads
Test: update → get → same value (no DB call needed for second get)

exercises.title: Razorpay merchant cache — full two-level synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Razorpay merchant config cache:
1. L1 Caffeine (200 merchants, 5min) + mock L2 behavior
2. Hit rate metrics (Counter by level)
3. Warm-up with top 50 merchants on startup
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): hit rate, warm-up count, cache invalidation works
5. All 3 pass
hints: Warm-up in @EventListener: runs after full Spring context is ready
hitRate after 2 hits: should be > 0 (some positive value)
l1.invalidate(key): removes from Caffeine; next get returns null

exercises.title: Cache monitoring dashboard PromQL
exercises.instructions:
Write Grafana dashboard queries for cache monitoring:
1. L1 cache hit rate over time (from Caffeine Gauge)
2. L2 Redis hit rate (from Counter ratio)
3. Cache miss rate per level (stacked panel)
4. Cache warm-up time tracking
5. Alert: L2 hit rate < 80% for 10 minutes

expected_stdout_contains: Cache dashboard: L1%, L2%, DB% hits over time

hints: L2 hit rate = l2_hits / (l2_hits + db_hits): how often Redis saves a DB call
Stacked panel: sum of all levels should equal total request rate
Alert < 80% L2: investigate cache size, TTL, or data pattern changes

exercises.title: Complete M22 T4 caching synthesis — L1 + metrics + warmup + invalidation
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete Razorpay payment gateway config cache:
1. L1 Caffeine (500, 5min, recordStats)
2. Hit rate Gauge + miss Counter
3. Warm-up @EventListener (top 100 gateway configs)
4. Cache invalidation on config update
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): warm-up count, hit rate, invalidation

All 3 tests pass.
hints: volatile warmedCount: visible across threads after EventListener writes
hitRate() after warm-up: cache has entries; first get = hit
invalidate → get → null: Caffeine removes key immediately
